ety: perf. part. of ālūdan; ā+rt. Z. rā = S. रा and ला
def: part. adj. Defiled, polluted, sullied, soiled, stained, spoiled; smeared, immersed, covered; loaded (with), overwhelmed.
more: adj. Ped. for H. lithṛā-huā . Spoiled; soiled; besmeared; polluted.
part. pass. (of آلودن) Defiled, stained, smeared, soiled; immersed, covered.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آلوده (ālūda), past participle of آلودن (ālūdan, “to pollute”).

def: s.f. Contamination, defilement, pollutedness; pollution, stain; foulness, impurity.
more: s. f. Contamination, stain, foulness, defilement, pollutedness.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آلُودَگِی (ālūdagī). First attested in c. 1649 as Middle Hindi آلودگی (ālvdgy /⁠ālūdagī⁠/).

oblique plural of آلُودَگی (ālūdagī)

part. adj. Astonished, demented, mad (with love, &c.), crazy ( = āshufta); — s.m. Reprobate, dissolute fellow; worthless companion. (Syn. rind.)
more: Pop. n. m. pass. part. of āluftan. lit . Astonished; out of one's mind. Strangers; foreigners; aliens ( gair ). Ghar-wāloṅ ko ṭukṛā nahīṅ juṛtā, bāhar ke alafte māl chakhte haiṅ . Mah. Wom. The household starve, while strangers feast.

s. plur. (of لون laun ) Sorts, kinds; various sorts.

more: n. prop. A high mountain, called also اروند arwand, in the vicinity of Hamadān.

def: Ped. n. f. A woman in childbirth; a lying in woman (during 40 days after delivery).

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian الیسع (alyasa’), from Arabic الْيَسَع (al-yasaʕ).

def: n. m. A. ال the, عبد slave. lit . the slave, or servant. The signature or initials of a subordinate officer or servant.
s.m. Mango, the fruit of the Mangifera indica; also the tree
more: n. m. S. आम्र , Pālī. ambo , Pr. ambam . A mango; fruit of the Mangifera Indica . 1. Ām ke ām, guṭhlȳoṅ ke dām . Prov. Eaten the mangoes, sold the seed. 2. Ām khāne, yā peṛ ginne? Prov. Will you eat the mangoes, or count the trees? (Said to one who asks impertinent questions). 3. Bāṛī meṅ bārah ām, saṭṭī meṅ aṭṭhārah ām! Prov. Twelve mangoes for a pice in the orchard, and eighteen in the market! 4. Sab ban ambvā maṅjarī gailo; Nandī, saṅīyāṅ nahīṅ aëlo . Song. Chait. The mango trees in blossom everywhere, My husband, sister, still away elsewhere. pāl kā ām, pāl kā laḍ'vā , n. m. A mango ripened in straw. Pāl kā ī laḍuā le . Street cry. ṭap'ke kā ām, ṭapkā , n. m. A ripe mango dropped from the tree.
s. m. The mango tree (Mangifera Indica); the mango fruit.
adj. Raw, undressed, unripe; s. m. Provisions, victuals (undressed).
subs: s.f. A tope or grove of mango trees; — income or revenue obtained from mango groves and gar dens
am-ras, s.m. Inspissated juice of the mango.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀅𑀁𑀩 (aṃba), from Sanskrit आम्र (āmrá).

def: adj. Raw, uncooked, undressed; unripe; undigested; — s.m. Condition of being raw, &c.; provisions, victuals (undressed); sickness, disease, (especially) disease in the bowels, the mucous matter voided in dysentery ( = āṅw, the common form)
subs: (S. āma+anna), s.m. Undressed rice, raw grain; uncooked provisions
(S. āma+vāta), s.m. Constipation or torpor of the bowels with flatulence and intumescence; — a windy tumour, a swelling (without pain)
s.m. Pain arising from indigestion; the colic.

ety: v.n. of āmadan; ā+Pehl. gmatan, rt. Zend gam = S. गम्
def: s.m. Coming, approach, arrival; access; coming in, income; receipts; — adj. Coming naturally, natural, unartificial, not studied (opp. to āward); plain, simple
more: n. m. P. آمدن to come. See اَمدني 1. Approach; coming ( ānā ); arrival. Āmad meṅ uskī mast hue kuchh khabar nahīṅ, Sāqī hai kaun, jām kahāṅ, aur kidhar shaāb? Drunk with delight that she would come, no sense had I, Where'er it be, of wine, or cup, or goblet-boy. āmad , adj. Opp. of āwurd far-fetched, artificial. 1. What comes naturally; natural; spontaneous; not studied or artificial (a composition). 2. Plain; simple; not laboured; idiomatic. āmad āmad ; H. āwan āwan , n. f. The news of one's coming. Āmad āmad hai kiskī gulshan meṅ, Gul jo phūle nahīṅ samāte haiṅ? Rind. Whose coming thrills the flower-buds so? The blooming flowers such gladness shew! āmad o kharch . Receipts and disbursements. اَمدووفت āmad o raft, āmad o shud , Ped.; āmad raft , Pop. n. m. 1. Coming and going; ingress and egress; communication ( ār jār ). 2. Passage; way; thoroughfare. 3. Intercourse; intercommunication…
subs: s.f. The announcement of an arrival
āmad o raft, s.f. Coming and going; ingress and egress, communication, intercourse; passage, way, thoroughfare
s.m. One who has a large income, a rich man; a merchant who purchases imported goods wholesale.

def: s. f. Approach.
s. f. Coming and going, ingress and egress, intercourse.

part. (of āmadan, 'to come'). Come, arrived.

ety: perf. part. of āmādan; ā+rt. Zend mā = S. मा
def: part. adj. Prepared, ready, alert; disposed (to)
more: adj. Prepared; ready. āmādah kar'nā , v. a. 1. To incite; rouse; stir up; excite (uksānā.) 2. To offer (to do an act); to abet. G. G. āmādah ho'nā , v. n. To be prepared; to get ready; hold oneself in readiness; be on the alert. Phr. To get into harness; to keep one's powder dry; grid one's loins; buckle on one's armour (musallah jaṅg honā). laṛāī ko āmādah ho'nā . To prepare for war.
subs: v.t. To get ready, to prepare; to rouse, stir up, incite, excite; to offer to do; to abet
v.n. To be prepared (for), get ready; to be on the alert; to be disposed (to).

def: s.f. The state of being prepared, readiness; preparation; alertness; — disposition (to); indication; threatening.
more: n. f. āmādah prepared. The state of being prepared; preparation; readiness; preparedness. — i-hamlah , A show of assault; threatening. — shar o fisād,—daṅgah , Intention to commit a breach of the peace apprehended.

def: s.m. One who has a large income, &c. = āmad-wālā, q.v.s.v. āmad.
s.m. A mound or heap of earth on which a mark is fixed to shoot arrows at, target, butt ( = nishāna).
more: s. m. A mound or heap of earth on which a mark is fixed to shoot arrows at, a mark or butt for shooting arrows at, an instrument with which land is ploughed, a throne, the twenty-fourth part of a parasang. آماجگاه āmāj-gāh, The place where the mark is fixed for shooting arrows at.

def: s.m. pl. (of makān), Dwellings, habitations, mansions, places.
more: s. plur. (of مکان) Mansions, habitations, places.

s.m. Hope, expectation.
more: adj. Pure, clean, clear, bright. امل پٿي a-mal-paṭṭī, A kind of stitching.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian امل (amal), from Arabic أَمَل (ʔamal).

def: adj. (f. -ā), Clean, pure; bright, clear.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian امل (amal), from Arabic أَمَل (ʔamal).

def: n. m. Intoxicating drinks or drugs; spirits; strong drinks. Kaho, bhaī ākā, softah hai, amal pānī kī thaihre? amal pānī kar'nā, yā jamānā , v. n. To take down, drink, or smoke any intoxicating drug; to liquor up; have a peg.
s.m. Register of one's doings or conduct; the register in which the deeds of men are supposed to be recorded.
more: (inf. iv. of عمل) s. Causing or teaching to act or operate.

def: s.m. Spirituous liquor, any intoxicating liquor or drug; intoxication
subs: s.m. Intoxicating liquor of any kind ( = amal).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian امل (amal), from Arabic أَمَل (ʔamal).

ety: inf. n. of عمل 'to work; to act,' &c.
def: s.m. Improper action; improper or bad use, misuse, abuse; misapplication, misemployment
more: n. m. عمل worked. 1. Practice; work; business; employment; operation ( ikhtyār , 8). 2. Act; deed; work; action; agency. 3. A charm; spell; a mystic word or incantation. 4. Measure; process; procedure. 5. Execution ( sādhan ). 6. Effect ( asar ). Abhī davā kā amal nahīṅ huā . 7. Administration ( amaldārī ). 8. Intoxicating drinks (امل پانی). 9. Time of day. Tīn kā amal hai . 10. An enema; a clyster. amal-i-bejā . 1. Abuse; ill use; misuse. 2. Misapplication; misemployment. aml paṛhnā , v. n. To mutter a spell or charm. amal-i-jarrāhī ; H. chīr phāṛ . A surgical operation. amal-dārī , n. f. 1. Limits of jurisdiction (حکومت). 2. Government; reign; lordship. 3. The district governed; a collectorship.

(Heb. ) s. m. Action, act, operation, work, practice, effect, rule, sway, jurisdiction. عملدار aﻌmal-dār, s. m. 1. One who has command. 2. A collector. عمل داري aﻌmal dār-ī, s. f. Collectorship, government. عمل کرنا aﻌmal-karnā, To act, operate, proceed.
subs: (-par), To pronounce a spell or incantation (over)
s.m. One who has command, or who exercises authority; an administrative officer; a (native) collector of revenue
s.f. Administration, government, rule, sway; lordship; limits of jurisdiction; the district governed, a collectorship
s.m. 'That which has come into operation'; a proceeding, process; — ʻamal-dar-āmad karnā, v.t. To put in practice, to act upon, to observe
v.n. To be put in practice, &c.
s.f. A warrant, a writ; a deed of conveyance; a certificate of title
(-par), To act (upon), proceed (upon); to operate, to take effect; to make use (of); — to affect the head (wine, &c.)
(-ko), To bring into operation or practice, put in execution, carry into effect; to make use of; to set to work
s.m. An order, a warrant, &c. ( = ʻamal-dastak); a code of instructions (cf. dastūruʼl-ʻamal).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عمل ('amal), from Arabic عَمَل (ʕamal).

def: (for A. عملة ʻamalat, pl. of ʻāmil, q.v.), s.m. pl. & sing. Workers, operators, executors; ministerial or public officers (attached to a court, or to an office), officials, functionaries; establishment (esp. the Persian or vernacular establishment of an office, as distinguished from the English department); — an official, a subordinate officer, a (native) clerk
more: n. m. 1. Establishment; office. The Persian or Vernacular establishment of an office, as distinguished from the English department; the ministerial officers attached to any office or court. 2. The materials of a broken house. amlah-pulis yā faujdārī . Police establishment. amlah-khurd . An inferior native clerk. amlah dukhlah k. , v. a. To bring under posssion
s. m. Operation, produce of work ¶ aﻌmala, 1. s. plur. (of عامل) Operators, executors, collectors of revenue, governours. 2. See عمل aﻌmal.
subs: s.m. pl. Ministerial officers, officials; executive authority; clerks.
wety: From Arabic عِمْلَة (ʕimla).

s. f. See آملا āmlā.
adv. In act; in fact, indeed, truly.
adj. Practical (opp. to ʻilmī, 'theoretical'); to be done, fabrile, fictitious, artificial: — addicted to intoxicating drugs.
more: adj. 1. Practical; not theoretical. 2. Addicted to intoxicating drugs. Jo main jāntī Bholā Nāth amlī, bagyā meṅ bhaṅgyā buāë rakhtī . Wom. Mahādeo kā biyāh. My simple sire a bhaṅgī had I known My garden with the drug I would have sown.
adj. Artificial, practical. حکمت عملي ḥikmat-i-aﻌmal-ī, Philosophy.

s.f. Practices.

v.n. To be affected with a tension of the veins of the belly, especially of the umbilical cord.

def: v.n. To become blunt or deadened by acidity (the teeth)
n. m. 1. Register; registry of conduct. 2. The book in which the good and evil deeds of men are supposed to be recorded.
s.f. A kind of stitching, a broad hem (resembling a leaf of the tamarind-tree in appearance).
more: n. f. A broad hem, so called from its resemblance to the tamarind leaf ( imlī kī pattī ).

def: s.f. A kind of stitching, a broad hem (resembling a leaf of the tamarind-tree in appearance).
s.m. The Indian Laburnum, Cathartocarpus (Cassia) fistula. (It is commonly used as a purgative).
more: n. m. Cathartocarpus fistula . Wat. used as a purgative. Khānā amaltās ho gayā . All he ate passed out as though he had taken a purgative.

def: s.f. Disinterestedness, unselfishness; indifference, stoicism.
s.f. Security, safety; freedom from fear, ease of mind; protection, safeguard; promise or assurance of security or safety, indemnity, quarter ( = panāh), mercy, grace
more: n. m. امن Being safe or secure. 1. Security; safety; impregnability. 2. Protection; tutelage; refuge; asylum ( panāh ); quarter. Jāo sidhāro, tumheṅ Allāh kī amān! 3. Tranquility; peace; quietude; calm. amān māṅg'nā , v. a. To ask protection from God. Jān kī amāṅ pāūṅ to arz karūṅ. amān meṅ rakh'nā , v. a. To render, or make safe; keep in safety; defend; guard; fence; shield; shelter; secure. Khudā apnī amān meṅ rakhe!
s. f. Safety, security, grace, mercy, protection, quarter.
subs: (-kī), To obtain assurance of safety (of), to find protection or grace
(-ko), To give a promise of safety, to afford protection, &c.
To seek protection, to seek the assurance of safety; to ask for quarter
v.t. To keep in safety or under (one's) protection, to render (one) secure; to protect, defend, shelter.

more: n. f. A large oval mango.
n. m. The winter rice crop, sown in July and August, and reaped in December.

ety: S. आम्र+इन्, lit. 'of or relating to the mango'; but cf. āwan
def: s.m. The rice crop which is sown in July (or the mango season), and reaped in December, the winter rice-crop

ety: आ or अ+S. मानीयं, rt. मा
def: v.n. To go into, fit in, to be contained, be held.
more: v. n. To hold; fit in; to be contained ( samānā ). Ek īṅṭ kā daṛbā, tis meṅ nau sau gāë amāë . Riddle. Ans. Ants. A one-brick stall, nine hundred creatures sprawl.
v. n. To be contained, to go into.

Fidelity; integrity; probity; good faith. 4. Entireness; integrity; ensemble . 5. Security; safety; protection. 6. In law . Safe conduct; safe-guard. 7. The office of an Amīn . amānat , adj. See joṅ kā toṅ. amānat bahī , n. f. Goods deposit book. amānat jārī , Assignments of revenue.

s. f. 1. Deposit, charge, any thing given in trust. 2. Faith, religion. امانت دار amānat-dār, Depositary, trustee, guardian, faithful, honest, trusty. امانت داري amānat-dār-ī, s. f. Guardianship, agency, trust, charge, faithfulness. امانت رکهنا amānat rakhnā, To deposit, to leave in charge or trust, to intrust.
subs: (orig. rel. adj. from amānat-ḵẖān, a proper name), s.f. A kind of dry tobacco used for chewing (syn. kaćaurī)
adj. Trustworthy, faithful, honest; — s.m. Guardian, custodian, trustee, depositary
s.f. Trustworthiness, &c. ( = amānat); — Charge, trust, guardianship, agency; custody, safe-keeping, deposit
(-ko), To deposit, consign, intrust, commit to the care (of); — to set or lay aside, put apart, leave out of account; to preserve intact; keep or hold as sacred or inviolable
s.m. Deed of trust or deposit; a document conveying anything.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَمَانَت (amānat), borrowed from Arabic أَمَانَة (ʔamāna), from أَمُنَ (ʔamuna).

def: n. m. Guardian; custodian; depositary; trustee. amānat-dārī , n. f. 1. Charge; trust; guardianship; agency. 2. Custody; safe-keeping; deposit. amānat rakhnā , v. a. To deposit; intrust; commit to the care of. amānat ke taur bhej'nā , G. G. To consign. amānat-guzār , n. m. A depositary with whom goods are deposited. amānat meṅ khyānat , Breach of trust. G. G.
s. f. Administration.
direct plural of اَمانَت (amānat)
adj. Deposited, given in trust or charge; — s.f. Any thing deposited, &c.
n. Depositary; depôt; ware-house; store-house. amānat khānī , n. f. A kind of tobacco chewed, not smoked.
A deed of trust or deposit; a document conveying anything.

vocative plural of اَمانَت (amānat)

adj. Unlucky, inauspicious, disastrous, evil; — s.m. Ill-luck; an evil omen; inauspiciousness; an unpleasantness, anything disagreeable, calamity, disaster.
adj. & s.m. Incredulous; inattentive; — without pride, humble; — an incredulous person, &c.
more: n. f. Government work in contradistinction to contract work. Amānī avādānī, ijārā ujāṛā . Prov. Prosperous amānī , ruin-stricken ijārā (lands in farm).
adj. 1. Incredulous, inattentive. 2. Without pride, humble.
s. f. 1. Security. 2. Trust, charge, deposit. 3. adj. (applied to land) What is in charge of a collector on the part of government (in opposition to اجاره that which is farmed).

def: adv. lit. 'We believe'; yea, verily.
adj. Unattractive; displeasing, disagreeable.
s.m. = amanoyog, q.v. s.m. Inattention.
adj. Disapproved reprobate.
s.m. Inattention.
adj. & s.m. Inattentive; — an inattentive person.
adj. & s.m. Unmanly; inhuman; — one who is not a man; a coward.
adj. Not human; anything but man; inhuman; monster.
adj. & s.m. Unmanly; inhuman; — one who is not a man; a coward.

ety: Zend rt. mar+prep. ā; S. आ+स्मर
def: s.m. Account, reckoning, calculation; investigation; — daily account-book, register of receipts and disbursements.
adj. Undying, exempt from death, immortal, everlasting; longlived; — an epithet of the Deity
more: n. m. Heaven; the region of the immortals or gods; paradise. amar honā, amar ho jānā , v. n. To become immortal; to live for ever; to enjoy everlasting life. Bābū dām kauṛī katek din khāūṅ, Dehū na asisyā, amar ho jāūṅ . Mag. Song. Sarwan . Good Sir, how long can riches life sustain, Your blessing give—eternal life I gain.
subs: (S. amar+valli), s.m. An epidendron, or parasitical plant similar to the misletoe
s.m. The state of an immortal, immortality
s.m. The region of the immortal, the region of the gods, heaven, paradise
v.n. To become immortal, to enjoy everlasting life; to be very longlived.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَمْر (amr), from Arabic أَمْر (ʔamr).

def: adj. S. अ not, मृ to die. Undying; immortal; everlasting.
q. v. S. अमर immortal, H. bel creeper. An epidendron, or parasitical plant similar to the mistletoe.

ety: P. انبار ambār or A. amār
def: s.m. Heap, pile. (The word would appear to be peculiar to the Panjāb.)
s.f. Immortality, deathlessness.
s. f. The celestial city of Indra.

ety: fr. A. عمار ʻamār, 'a turban; a crown,' &c.; rt. عمر 'to build,' &c.
def: s.f. A litter or seat with a canopy, to ride in (on an elephant; — when it has no canopy it is called a hauda)
more: n. f. A litter or rider's seat with a canopy on an elephant. Without a canopy it is a haudā . Uṭh Bharat Rām milan āë, sone kā haudā, zarad ambārī, Upar chaṅvar dhurat āë . Bharat-milāvā.
s. f. A litter or seat with a canopy to ride in on an elephant or camel. (When it has no canopy, it is called هوده).
subs: adj. Having a canopied litter on its back (an elephant).

def: s.m. pl. (of amrad), Beardless youths.

def: s.f. = amī, q.v. s.f. Water of life, nectar; water, rain.
adj. Undying, imperishable.
adj. (compar. & superl. of majd), More, or most, glorious.
s. m. Trifles, trifling; a person of little estimation.

n. m. अमुक a certain person or thing = A. fulāṅ , Pālī amuko . 1. This one and that one; great and small; every one. 1. Maiṅ imke-ḍhimke kī nahīṅ suntā . 2. Būṛhā, adheṛ, imkā ḍhimkā, falāṅ, kyā hai? Ham se jo ho muqābil, patṭhe meṅ jān kyā hai? The old man boastful. Nazīr. Old though in years, yet vig'rous still to strike, Breathes there the man who dare with me contend, Old, middle-ag'd, this one or that belike! Tush! What's a young man's strength to mine, my friend!

ety: S. अमुक+redup. formed from S. base त
def: s.m. This one and that one, small and great, every one (promiscuously); a nobody (a vague term of contempt used in speaking of a person); trifling, trifles, baubles; idle talk, prating.

ety: S. अमुक+redup. formed from S. base त
def: s.m. This one and that one, small and great, every one (promiscuously); a nobody (a vague term of contempt used in speaking of a person); trifling, trifles, baubles; idle talk, prating.

def: s.m. The plant Emblic Myrobalan, Phyllanthus emblica (commonly called آنولا āṅwlā).

def: adj. Sour, acid; — s.m. Sourness, acidity.

ety: prob. for A. عملة ʻamilat; or fr. ʻamal
def: s.m. Work, &c. ( = ʻamal); a thing done; premises, buildings, &c.; materials (of a dilapidated house).
wety: From Arabic عِمْلَة (ʕimla).

def: s.m. pl. (of ʻamla), Public officials; subordinate officers, clerks; managers.
s.f. pl. (of milk), Possessions, goods, estates, property, lands.
more: s. f. plur. (of ملک q. v.) Possessions, properties (generally applied to land). ¶ imlāk (inf. iv. of ملک Heb. and Ch. ) s. m. Causing to possess.

def: adj. Clean, pure; clear, bright; — s.m. Globe-amaranth, Gomphræna globosa.
s.f. Sourness, acidity.
s.f. The tamarind tree. (See imlī.)
usually; generally; normally
wety: Combination of عامّ ('āmm, “common, general, ordinary”) + طَور (taur, “manner”) + postposition سے (se, “from, by”).

ety: act. part. of عمّ 'to be common,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Common, general, universal; public; vulgar, popular; plebeian; ordinary; generally or universally comprehensive (of -se), not restricted, including, inclusive (of); — the vulgar, the common people, the mass
more: (from عمّ Heb. To cover, to contain; and, hence, to be common) adj. 1. Common, publick, general, plebeian. 2. The vulgar, the common people. خاص وعام ḵẖāss-o-āﻌmm, High and low, peculiar and popular.
subs: adj. Generally intelligible; popular (as a treatise)
adj. (f. -ī), Of or relating to the public, or to the masses; public
s.m. pl. The common people, &c. ( = ʻām)
High and low, gentle and simple, rich and poor.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عَامّ (āmm), borrowed from Arabic عَامّ (ʕāmm).

def: s.m. A father s brother, paternal uncle
more: (Heb. or Connexion, propinquity) s. m. An uncle, fatherʼs brother. عم زاده aﻌmm-zāda, Paternal uncleʼs son.

def: mom, mother; mommy, mummy
wety: From Sanskrit अम्बा (ambā).

def: conj. But, moreover, nevertheless, however, yet.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اما (ammā), from Arabic أَمَّا (ʔammā).

def: s.f. Mother ( = mā, māṅ); — intj. (used by women), My dear! darling!
more: n. f. S. अम्बा, , Pālī. ammā , A. اُم a mother. Mother; dear mother; mamma. am'mā! intj. Wom. My dear; my darling; my soul. Ammā, dauṛ-ke na chal, gir paṛegā . Mah. Wom. ammā jān , Mah.; mā jī , Hin. n. f. A mother-in-law ( sās ).
conj. But, moreover, nevertheless, however, yet.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اما (ammā), from Arabic أَمَّا (ʔammā).

def: s.f. = ammā, q.v. s.f. Mother ( = mā, māṅ); — intj. (used by women), My dear! darling!
more: s. f. See اما ammā, Mother.

ety: P. انبار ambār or A. amār
def: s.m. Heap, pile. (The word would appear to be peculiar to the Panjāb.)
(for A. امارة ammārat, fem. of ammār), adj. m.f. Wont to command; imperious, domineering, headstrong, obstinate. (See nafs-i-ammāra, s.v. nafs.)

s.f. = amāvas, q.v. s.f. The night during which the moon and sun are in conjunction; the first day of the first quarter on which the moon is invisible; the night of new moon; the last day of the dark fortnight.
s.f. One of the ten kinds of wounds compensated by a fine.
s.m. Father's brother, paternal uncle (cf. ḵẖālū, fr. ḵẖāl).
more: n. m. Father's brother; paternal uncle. ammū-zādah ; H. chacherā bhāī , n. m. Father's brother's son; cousin.

def: s.m. Security, safety; tranquillity, peace
more: n. m. See amān (2, 3.). Security; safety. aman-chain , Mah. Wom.; zalzalā Ped.; hālan- ḍolan . Rus. n. m. Earthquake ( bhoṅchāl ). aman meṅ rakhnā , v. n. To preserve; keep in safety, peace, comfort, security, etc. amn o āmān, aman-āmān, aman chain , n. m. Peace and tranquility.
s. m. Security, safety. امن امان amn amān, s. m. Quarter, crying out for quarter.
subs: amn o amān, s.m. Security and tranquillity; — adv. In safety and peace
v.t. To keep in safety, &c., to take care of, preserve
wety: Borrowed from Arabic أَمْن (ʔamn).

def: v.n. (prov.) = ānā, q.v. s.m. Postponement; delay; opportune or seasonable moment; opportunity
subs: adj. Procrastinating, dilatory; time-serving; seeking opportunity; — s.m. Dilatory person; time-server.

def: s.m. Confronting, opposition; front; — adj. Facing, opposite
subs: adv. Opposite, face to face, in front
v.t. To confront, face; to place before or in front; to bring face to face; to put side by side, to compare.

def: (from آمنا سامنا q. v. adv. Opposite, face to face, over against.

prep. Opposite; face to face; facing; vis-a-vis. ām'ne sāmne kar'nā , v. a. 1. To confront; bring face to face; place side by side, or in juxta-position. 2. To bring together for comparison ( milānā ); compare ( mutābiq k .). Āmne sāmne ghar karūṅ, aur bich karūṅ maidān . I will take up my abode right in front, and there shall be no screen between us. (Said to an impudent, shameless woman).
see s.v. āmnā-sāmnā. s.m. Confronting, opposition; front; — adj. Facing, opposite
subs: adv. Opposite, face to face, in front
v.t. To confront, face; to place before or in front; to bring face to face; to put side by side, to compare.

def: adj. Unerring, unfailing; effectual; infallible; genuine, real; productive, fruitful.

ety: perf. part. of āmoḵẖtan; ā+rt. Zend muć = S. मुच्
def: part. adj. Taught, learned; — s.m. That which has been learned; an old lesson
more: n. m. P. آموختن to teach, or learn. What has been read or learnt; old lessons. āmokhtah paṛhnā, yā phernā , v. n. To revise, read over again.
subs: To read over old lessons, to revise.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آموخته (āmoxta), past participle of آموختن (âmoxtan, “to teach; learn”). Previously attested in 1649.

def: adj. Priceless, invaluable, unpurchaseable, very rare or precious; super-excellent, beautiful, lovely, exquisite.
more: adj. S. अमूल्य from अ not, मूल्य price. 1. Priceless; invaluable; very rare or precious. 1. Ghuṛlā to bāko hai mol amolā, Aur sugaṛ hai tā pe kāṭhī bāṅdhī, Bākī kārī kārī julpheṅ mere man meṅ basīṅ . Priceless his horse without a counterpart, Such lovely trappings too, so gorgeous, smart, His black black locks have taken quite my heart. 2. Is tan bhītar lāl amolak, chhin chhin jāë ghaṭā ghaṭā, Jab yeh kansā bhayā purānā, sab ko lāge phaṭā phaṭā . Bhajan. Within this frame's a priceless gem, each moment wasting, wasting, Time-worn, to every eye it seems a torn and tattered thing. 2. Superexcellent; beautiful; lovely; exquisite. Hārī sārī guālaneṅ, aur dīnā ghūṅgaṭ khol, Khusī hue bahut Krishn jī, dekhe daras amol . Rās līlā. Tired out at length the milkmaids did their faces all unveil, Admitted to the lovely sight did Krishn his sense regale.

def: adj. Invaluable.

ety: S. आम्र+क+dim. aff. र; Pr. अम्बो+लको; — o-lā is a com. dim. aff. in the modern Aryan tongues
def: s.m. Seedling of a mango-plant. (It is rubbed down till an opening is made, and is blown, like a pipe or whistle, by children.)
W. n. m. The soft kernel of a young mango tree, played upon by boys as on a pipe. Merā amol baje, bībī kā ḍhol baje . E. Song. My mango stone I pipe, the lady beats her drum.

n. m. Commands and prohibitions; orders and counter orders.

adj. Beardless; handsome (a youth). امردپرست amrad-parast, A sodomite.

def: s.f. = ām-rāʼī, q.v.s.v. īm.
s.f. A mango-grove ( = ām-rāʼī and ambrāʼī, q.q.v.).
s. f. A garden of mango trees.
n. f. Revenue derived from gardens and mango groves.

n. f. आम्रराजि from आम्र, राजि a row. H. ām mango, Mango groves. Chal na, amraīyoṅ meṅ jhūleṅ leṅ darakhtoṅ kī hawā . Inshā. Come with me, Love, to the mango grove, we'll swing together by the breezes fanned.
s.m. = ām-ras, q.v.s.v. ām.
